# terrible_community_general_ssh_config (Data Source)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host` (String) The endpoint this configuration is valid for. It can be an actual address on the internet or an alias that connects to the value of `hostname`.
- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this data source against

### Optional

- `add_keys_to_agent` (Boolean) Sets the `AddKeysToAgent` option.
- `controlmaster` (String) Sets the `ControlMaster` option.
- `controlpath` (String) Sets the `ControlPath` option.
- `controlpersist` (String) Sets the `ControlPersist` option.
- `dynamicforward` (String) Sets the `DynamicForward` option.
- `forward_agent` (Boolean) Sets the `ForwardAgent` option.
- `group` (String) Which group this configuration file belongs to. If none given, `user` is used.
- `host_key_algorithms` (String) Sets the `HostKeyAlgorithms` option.
- `hostname` (String) The actual host to connect to when connecting to the host defined.
- `identities_only` (Boolean) Specifies that SSH should only use the configured authentication identity and certificate files (either the default files, or those explicitly configured in the `ssh_config` files or passed on the ssh command-line), even if `ssh-agent` or a `PKCS11Provider` or `SecurityKeyProvider` offers more identities.
- `identity_file` (String) The path to an identity file (SSH private key) that is used when connecting to this host. File need to exist and have mode `0600` to be valid.
- `other_options` (String) Allows specifying arbitrary SSH config entry options using a dictionary. The key names must be lower case. Keys with upper case values are rejected. The values must be strings. Other values are rejected.
- `port` (String) The actual port to connect to when connecting to the host defined.
- `proxycommand` (String) Sets the `ProxyCommand` option. Mutually exclusive with `proxyjump`.
- `proxyjump` (String) Sets the `ProxyJump` option. Mutually exclusive with `proxycommand`.
- `remote_user` (String) Specifies the user to log in as.
- `ssh_config_file` (String) SSH config file. If `user` and this option are not specified, `/etc/ssh/ssh_config` is used. Mutually exclusive with `user`.
- `state` (String) Whether a host entry should exist or not.
- `strict_host_key_checking` (String) Whether to strictly check the host key when doing connections to the remote host. The value `accept-new` is supported since community.general 8.6.0.
- `user` (String) Which user account this configuration file belongs to. If none given and `ssh_config_file` is not specified, `/etc/ssh/ssh_config` is used. If a user is given, `~/.ssh/config` is used. Mutually exclusive with `ssh_config_file`.
- `user_known_hosts_file` (String) Sets the user known hosts file option.

### Read-Only

- `hosts_added` (String) A list of host added.
- `hosts_change_diff` (String) A list of host diff changes.
- `hosts_changed` (String) A list of host changed.
- `hosts_removed` (String) A list of host removed.
